title: Interior Department requests information on offshore launch options
summary: The U.S. Department of the Interior is seeking information on offshore orbital launch concepts to reduce congestion at existing spaceports. This effort is part of a broader initiative to expand launch capabilities beyond traditional land-based facilities.

title: SDA TAP Lab Evolves Into BMC3I TAP Lab And In Partnership With Catalyst Campus Launches A New Multi-Phased Program To Accelerate Mission-Focused Technology Development
summary: The SDA TAP Lab has been rebranded as the BMC3I TAP Lab and, in partnership with Catalyst Campus, launched a multi-phased program to accelerate mission-focused technology development through collaboration between commercial innovators and government entities.
company: Catalyst Campus for Technology and Innovation (CCTI)

title: The Exploration Company establishes US entity to pursue government contracts
summary: The Exploration Company, a German space transportation firm, has created a US-based subsidiary named TEC Federal to compete for government contracts. This move aims to expand its market access in the United States.

title: Environmental groups urge FCC to pause orbital data center applications
summary: Environmental and scientific groups are urging the FCC to pause processing applications for orbital data centers, citing concerns over the environmental impact and lack of oversight. The push aligns with broader demands for stricter regulations on megaconstellations.

title: Xona unveils program to verify Pulsar-compatible devices
summary: Xona, a Silicon Valley startup, announced a program to verify devices compatible with its Pulsar precision positioning, navigation, and timing (PNT) constellation. The company revealed a high-profile roster of partners developing compatible equipment.

title: Volatility is often the price of ambition
summary: The article discusses the volatility in SpaceX's stock during its early IPO days, drawing parallels to the challenges faced by constellations designed around Starship. It highlights the risks associated with ambitious space industry ventures.

title: Maintaining leadership in space with Victoria Coleman
summary: Victoria Coleman, former chief scientist for the U.S. Air Force, discusses maintaining leadership in space on the Space Minds podcast. The episode highlights her current role as associate provost and insights on space leadership.

title: Space Force completes procurement reorganization, creating nine acquisition portfolios
summary: The U.S. Space Force has completed a procurement reorganization, establishing nine acquisition portfolios. Each portfolio will be managed by new executives responsible for requirements and modernization decisions across key mission areas.

title: Space Force awards Pulse Space $40 million to advance laser power technology
summary: The U.S. Space Force has awarded Pulse Space a $40 million contract to advance laser power technology for transmitting power and data between spacecraft. The startup is focused on developing innovative laser systems to enhance space operations.

title: Golden Dome and the search for new space markets
summary: Golden Dome, an unsettled missile-defense program, is becoming a key focus for space and defense startups seeking new markets. The initiative highlights opportunities for innovation in the space industry.

title: Wally Funk, trailblazing pilot and astronaut, passes away at 87
summary: Wally Funk, a pioneering pilot and astronaut who flew to space at age 82, has passed away at 87. She was a lifelong advocate for women in aviation and aerospace.

title: Scientists have discovered the oldest quasar ever seen, and it shines with the light of a trillion suns
summary: Scientists discovered the oldest quasar ever observed using ESA's Euclid space telescope, which shines with the brightness of a trillion suns. The quasar, powered by a black hole, is the most ancient and distant ever recorded.

title: SpaceX wants to launch 100,000 Starlink satellites to orbit
summary: SpaceX has applied to launch a massive constellation of 100,000 next-generation Starlink satellites, each weighing around 4,400 pounds. This marks a significant expansion of their existing Starlink network.

title: 'Reckless' space-based data centers lack environmental review, drawing criticism
summary: Critics argue that allowing a million space-based data centers without environmental review is irresponsible. The move has drawn significant backlash.

title: 'Silo' season 3 showrunner Graham Yost explains the time jumps and turning half of the show into a political thriller (interview)
summary: The showrunner of 'Silo' season 3, Graham Yost, discusses the narrative choices of time jumps and a shift toward a political thriller tone, drawing comparisons to 1970s films like 'Three Days of The Condor' and 'The Parallax View'.

title: Could evidence of life on Mars be hiding in clay? Europe wants to send a rover to check
summary: Europe plans to send ESA's Rosalind Franklin rover to Mars to investigate potential signs of life in Martian clay. The mission aims to analyze clay deposits which may preserve evidence of past biological activity.

title: Take a trip to the turquoise waters of the Bahamas (from space) | Space photo of the day for July 9, 2026
summary: A space photo showcasing the turquoise waters of the Bahamas was featured as the space photo of the day for July 9, 2026. The image highlights the stunning beauty of the region from space.

title: The US wants to build offshore rocket launch sites. Critics say 'our coasts deserve better'
summary: The U.S. government is exploring the possibility of building offshore rocket launch sites for commercial launches. Critics argue that this could turn the ocean into a 'dumping ground.'

title: China announces plan to build early-warning system for dangerous asteroids
summary: China revealed plans to develop an early-warning system for dangerous asteroids, including spacecraft to detect asteroids approaching from the sun's direction, a previously overlooked blind spot in space-rock monitoring.

title: Staffing the moon base: How many astronauts should live in NASA's lunar outpost?
summary: A simulation study suggests that mission design, not just psychology, is crucial for the success of NASA's future lunar outpost. The research explores optimal astronaut staffing levels for a sustainable moon base.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for Astronaut Anil Menon Launch to Space Station
summary: NASA astronaut Anil Menon will launch aboard the Roscosmos Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft to the International Space Station on July 14, joining Expedition 74 to advance scientific research.

title: NASA Space Telescope Maps Magnetic Fields of ‘Lighthouse’ Pulsar
summary: NASA's IXPE has directly measured the magnetic fields of the pulsar PSR J1101−6101 within the Lighthouse Nebula. The findings offer new insights into the structure of extreme cosmic objects.

title: Principal Investigator and Quality Assessment Reports Evaluate Umbra Synthetic Aperture Radar Data
summary: Principal Investigator and Quality Assessment Reports evaluate Umbra's Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) data, highlighting its contributions to Earth science research and applications. The findings underscore the growing role of commercial data in scientific studies.

title: Curiosity Sees Martian Sulfur Up Close
summary: NASA's Curiosity Mars rover discovered fragments of sulfur crystals on the Red Planet after accidentally crushing a rock on May 30, 2024. This marks the first time such crystals have been observed on Mars.

title: NASA Scientists Take to Air and Space to Study Arctic Sea Ice
summary: NASA engineers are testing a spacecraft sensor to measure Arctic sea ice loss, with field research conducted in the Canadian wilderness ahead of its planned launch next year.

title: Super Typhoon Bavi
summary: Super Typhoon Bavi, the third Category 5 tropical cyclone of 2026, impacted the U.S. Northern Mariana Islands and Guam before heading toward Asia. This severe weather event highlights the increasing intensity of tropical storms in the region.

title: Students Connect NASA Science With Indigenous Knowledge to Study Coastal Erosion
summary: Students are collaborating with NASA to integrate Indigenous knowledge with scientific methods to study coastal erosion affecting the Pleasant Point Passamaquoddy Reservation. The project aims to address rapid shoreline changes and historical land loss in the region.

title: Hubble Captures Star-Studded Cluster
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of Messier 3 (M3), a massive globular cluster in the Milky Way galaxy. M3 is composed of ancient stars formed at the same time from a single gas cloud, making them similar in age.

title: NASA Transfers ‘Hundred Acre Wood’ to Patuxent Research Refuge
summary: NASA ceremonially transferred ownership of 105 acres of wooded land at its Goddard Space Flight Center to the Patuxent Research Refuge, managed by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service. The land, formerly known as Area 400, is now part of the largest unfragmented forest block in the region.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the development of its Neutron rocket faced delays. The company reported significant gains in Electron launches during this period.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: The Space Coast is seeing significant infrastructure growth with new launch pads being built for Stoke Space and Relativity Space. This marks a continued revitalization of the region's space industry capabilities.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debuts of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets. Three Falcon 9 flights are also expected from SpaceX.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch with multiple milestones achieved. The station aims to serve as a private orbital outpost.

title: Starship in Florida pushing for launch this year
summary: SpaceX is accelerating preparations for Starship launches from Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year. The company and its contractors are making progress toward this goal.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar landers for its Moon Base Phase One initiative in June 2026. This marks a significant step in advancing lunar exploration and establishing a sustainable human presence on the Moon.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral using a hybrid plan following a recent New Glenn anomaly that caused significant damage. The company aims to restore operations at the launch site promptly.

title: Launch Preview: Swift rescue mission, final Atlas satellite launch
summary: A rescue mission for the Swift observatory is planned alongside the final launch of an Atlas satellite. Multiple orbital launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus XL rocket is scheduled for its first mission since 2021 to air launch the Swift Boost Mission, aiming to save a NASA space telescope.

title: Euclid studies Milky Way center, supports future Roman observations
summary: ESA's Euclid space telescope captured a new image of the Milky Way's center, which will aid future observations by NASA's Roman Space Telescope. The data supports scientific research and mission planning for both telescopes.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, though the specific details were not provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the CM25 meeting, detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities across ESA programmes.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for space applications, highlighting its versatility and potential impact on future missions. The innovation is positioned as a significant advancement in space robotics technology.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific research.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. This initiative supports European space science by involving research institutes in space missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions, supported by young researchers. Their work is crucial for the success of European Space Agency missions.

title: Blue Origin Seeks $10B at $130B Valuation, Reporting Shows
summary: Blue Origin is seeking $10 billion in funding at a $130 billion valuation, signaling the end of its reliance on Jeff Bezos' personal funding. The move indicates a shift toward external investment for the space company.

title: Auxilium Demos Tech To Enable Sustainable Bioprinting in Orbit
summary: Auxilium Biotechnologies successfully created multiple types of organ tissues aboard the ISS, demonstrating scalable bioprinting technology in orbit. This achievement highlights future applications for sustainable bioprinting in space.

title: ispace Launches Lunar Integrator Service With Starship
summary: ispace has secured 500 kg of payload capacity on SpaceX's Starship for a lunar mission launching no earlier than 2030. This marks the company's entry into a new lunar integrator service.

title: Venus Aerospace Raises $91M Series B to Scale RDRE Production
summary: Venus Aerospace has raised $91 million in a Series B funding round to scale production of its Rotating Detonation Rocket Engine (RDRE) technology. This investment highlights growing industry interest in the more efficient rocket propulsion method.

title: FCC Poised To Advance Sat Licensing Reform
summary: The FCC is set to vote on the Space Modernization Order on July 22, aiming to reform satellite licensing processes. FCC Chair Brendan Carr announced this initiative last week.

title: Transporter-17 Sends the First Commercial Nuclear Sat to Orbit
summary: SpaceX's Transporter-17 rideshare mission launched 81 payloads to LEO, including the first commercial nuclear satellite. The mission lifted off from Vandenberg Space Force Base at 3:12 AM.

title: NASA Releases CLD Draft RFP
summary: NASA is advancing its plan to replace the ISS by releasing a draft RFP for Commercial Low Earth Orbit (CLD) stand-alone replacements. The move follows industry success in developing human habitation solutions in Low Earth Orbit.

title: Exclusive: Ravee Optics Raises $6M Seed to Build Compact Laser Terminals
summary: Ravee Optics has raised $6 million in a seed funding round to develop compact laser communication terminals. The company aims to make laser communications more accessible with lighter, smaller, and scalable terminals.

title: The Biggest Fundraises of H1 2026
summary: The article discusses the largest fundraises in the first half of 2026 within the space industry, highlighting significant financial growth. It emphasizes how increased investment is enabling advancements across various space-related sectors.

title: Inside the Deepening Ties Between Europe and the Middle East
summary: Europe's strained geopolitical ties with the US have led to stronger space industry collaborations with Middle Eastern companies. This shift is creating new opportunities for partnerships in the sector.

title: MDA Space Vertically Integrates in EO Analytics With Deal to Acquire Majority of CLS
summary: MDA Space is acquiring a majority stake in French Earth observation data analytics company CLS to vertically integrate in EO analytics. This deal strengthens MDA Space's position in the Earth observation value chain.

title: SpaceX Files for 100,000-Satellite Gen-3 Constellation
summary: SpaceX has submitted a filing with the FCC to launch a third-generation satellite constellation consisting of 100,000 satellites for high-speed internet connectivity. The constellation aims to operate in W- and D-band frequencies.

title: Telesat to Provide Mil-Ka Arctic Connectivity for Canada’s ESCP-P Program
summary: Telesat has secured a deal with the Canadian government to supply Military Ka-band satellite capacity for Canada's Enhanced Satellite Communications Project – Polar (ESCP-P) program. The agreement aims to enhance Arctic connectivity for military communications.

title: Rivada Signs Connectivity Partner in Nepal
summary: Rivada Space Networks has partnered with a telecoms company in Nepal to provide connectivity services. The deal involves Mercantile Space, a communications technology firm in the country with a population of around 30 million.

title: Impulse Space and Relativity Space Join the Field for Space Force NSSL Phase 3, Lane 1
summary: Impulse Space and Relativity Space have been added to the list of eligible companies for the U.S. Space Force's National Security Space Launch (NSSL) Phase 3, Lane 1 awards. The field now includes seven companies competing for these contracts.

title: Beyond Gravity Signs Gamechanging Contract with Ariane Group
summary: Beyond Gravity has signed a major contract with ArianeGroup, solidifying its role in Europe's launch supply chain. This deal is one of the largest in Beyond Gravity's history.

title: Iceye Makes Major Move in German With New Business Entity
summary: Iceye has established a new business entity in Germany, based in Berlin and Munich, to expand its presence in the German space market. This move highlights Iceye's strategic interest in tapping into the dynamic German space industry.

title: York Space and All.Space CEOs Detail Resilient Communications Vision as Acquisition Closes
summary: York Space Systems completed its acquisition of satcom terminal company All.Space to create a complete tactical satcom ecosystem. The CEOs of both companies highlighted the vision for resilient communications following the deal.

title: CesiumAstro Files for 737-Satellite Synchronicity Constellation
summary: CesiumAstro has filed an FCC application for a 737-satellite constellation named Synchronicity to offer reconfigurable connectivity for fixed and mobile satellite users. The Austin-based company specializes in software-defined radio technology.

title: NATO Allies Plan Hybrid HALO Constellation
summary: Eight NATO allies are collaborating to create a hybrid military satellite constellation named HALO to enhance NATO's space-based military capabilities and resilience. The initiative aims to integrate national military satellites into a unified system.
